name: Lakako Lasterkaldia – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Lakako Lasterkaldia en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
Starting from the small village of Ossès, the Laka race offers a loop of 21 kilometers and about 1,200 meters of elevation gain. In the heart of the Basque Country, in the province of Basse-Navarre, this village is ideally located between Bidarray, Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry and Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port.

The course starts from the center of the village then rises gradually on a track. You do most of the elevation on your first ascent, which really begins after just over a kilometer.

A little before the fifth kilometer, you come out on a beautiful ridge marked by the presence of ruins. You bypass the Haltzamendi and then climb the Laina. You evolve on a new ridge which then leads you to Mount Baïgura, the highest point of your outing at 897 meters above sea level. Savor for a moment the magnificent panorama that opens on the ridges of Iparla, Artzamendi and Rhune.

The route ends with a long and beautiful descent that brings you back to Ossès. You follow several pretty little streams until you find your starting point.

Usually the race takes place during the last week of April. You can find more information here :
https://coursedulaka.net/
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ossès offer a diverse landscape for runners, situated in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques region. The area features mountainous terrain with peaks like Artzamendi and Baïgura, providing varying elevations for challenging runs. The Laka stream and Nive River contribute to tranquil riverside paths, while extensive meadows and forests cover much of the territory. This natural environment provides a wide array of trails suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Ossès The most popular jogging route is Lakako Lasterkaldia – running in the Basque Country, a 12.4 miles (19.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Basque Country mountains. Another top favourite among local runners is Laina loop from Ossès, a difficult 8.7 miles (14.1 km) path. This route offers scenic views across the mountainous landscape surrounding Ossès. Local runners also love the Baïgura – Below Erregelu loop from Ossès, a 13.5 miles (21.8 km) trail leading through varied mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Jogging around Ossès is defined by mountainous terrain, tranquil riversides, and extensive green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Ossès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Nearly 6000 runners have used komoot to explore Ossès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy running paths in Ossès for beginners?
A: Yes, while many routes in Ossès are challenging, there is at least one easy running route available. The region offers a diverse network of trails, and you can find options suitable for different fitness levels, including those just starting out.

Q: Can I find short jogging trails near Ossès?
A: The running routes around Ossès tend to be on the longer side, with the shortest listed being approximately 10.9 km (6.8 miles). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41517768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Halcamendy Peak loop from Ossès&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.9 km path. While these might not be considered 'short' by all, they offer a good option for a quicker run compared to longer mountain trails.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in the Ossès area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Ossès are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26273492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laina loop from Ossès&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29637785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baïgura – Below Erregelu loop from Ossès&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic views and varied terrain.

Q: What are some scenic running routes around Ossès?
A: Ossès is renowned for its picturesque landscapes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26273492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laina loop from Ossès&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning views across the mountainous Basque Country. The entire region is characterized by extensive meadows, forests, and the tranquil Laka stream, providing a beautiful backdrop for your run.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Ossès suitable for families?
A: While many routes in Ossès are rated as difficult due to significant elevation gain, the region does offer a variety of trails. For families, it's best to look for the easier paths, which provide a more gentle experience. The extensive green spaces and tranquil riverside areas can be pleasant for a family outing, but always check the specific route's difficulty before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Ossès?
A: The natural environment of Ossès, with its extensive meadows and forests, is generally welcoming for dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near protected wildlife. Always check local regulations or specific route details for any restrictions.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls or rivers?
A: Yes, the Laka stream gracefully traverses the village of Ossès, and its banks offer pleasant routes for leisurely jogs. The Laka also features small waterfalls, providing a refreshing and scenic element to your run. The nearby Nive River also contributes to tranquil riverside paths.

Q: What kind of attractions can I see while jogging in Ossès?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Ossès offers cultural and historical points of interest. You can enjoy scenic runs through the village itself, appreciating its 18th-century Bas-Navarrais style houses. The surrounding area also features impressive mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iparla Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1365446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Artzamendi&lt;/a&gt;, and mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ispéguy Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which can be seen or incorporated into longer runs.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Ossès?
A: The running routes in Ossès are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Nearly 6,000 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging ascents, rewarding panoramic views, and the overall natural beauty of the Basque Country mountains.

Q: How many running routes are there in Ossès?
A: There are 13 running routes available around Ossès, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These include 1 easy, 3 moderate, and 9 difficult trails.

Q: Is Ossès a good destination for challenging mountain runs?
A: Absolutely. Ossès is an excellent destination for challenging mountain runs, situated amidst mythical Basque Country peaks like Artzamendi, Baïgura, and the Iparla ridges. Many routes feature significant elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045525363/lakako-lasterkaldia-running-in-the-basque-country" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakako Lasterkaldia – running in the Basque Country&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 800 meters of ascent over 19.9 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Ossès?
A: Ossès offers beautiful running conditions throughout much of the year. The extensive green spaces and forests provide a refreshing escape in warmer months, while the mountainous terrain can be invigorating in cooler seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. For winter running, be prepared for varying conditions in the mountains.

Q: Are there any running routes that include historical or cultural sights?
A: Yes, jogging through the village of Ossès itself allows you to appreciate its rich architectural heritage, including well-preserved 18th-century Bas-Navarrais style houses. The region's proximity to charming villages like Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port, known for its historical significance, also offers opportunities to combine running with cultural exploration.
tips: The Laina is located on a pretty ridge line at 869 meters above sea level. It is located between the Haltzamendi and the Baïgura.
Mount Baïgura culminates at 897 meters in the province of Labourd. Its position set back from the chain offers an exceptional belvedere on the Pyrenees.
Ossès or "Ortzaitze" in Basque is a charming little village ideally located. Located at the foot of Mount Baïgura, it is close to Bidarray, Saint-Étienne-de-Baïgorry and Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port.
